new_classes.label_en: Rum and Other Spirits from Fermented Sugar-Cane Products
new_classes.label_de: Rum und andere Spirituosen aus vergorenem Zuckerrohr
new_classes.definition_en: Spirits obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (including rum and tafia) that retain their organoleptic characteristics, classified under CN subheadings 2208 40 11 to 2208 40 99 (Chapter 22).

Process of fermenting sugar-cane products and subsequently distilling the fermented material to produce rum, tafia, or other sugar-cane-derived spirits; each individual represents the unique sugar-cane distillation process type in the CN Chapter 22 classification.

new_classes.label_en: Rum and Sugar-Cane Spirit in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22084051)
new_classes.label_de: Rum und Zuckerrohrspirituosen in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter (KN 22084051)
new_classes.definition_en: Rum and other spirits obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, retaining their organoleptic characteristics, classified under CN code 2208 40 51 (Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for CN 22084051: (1) the product is a spirit (subclass of eucn:Spirit), (2) it is obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (SugarCaneDistillation process), (3) it retains organoleptic characteristics (retainsOrganolepticalCharacteristics = true), and (4) the container volume is ≤ 2 L (RumSmallContainer). The reference to the HS Explanatory Note third paragraph (3) for rum and tafia is noted but cannot be formally axiomatised without introducing a named individual for "rum" and "tafia" as sub-types, which would require additional legal text not directly available. The disjointness between SugarCaneDistillation and GrapeDistillation is captured via a complement restriction as a proxy; full pairwise disjointness with all other distillation/production process classes (GrainDistillation, MaltFermentation, etc.) is partially implied by the class hierarchy but not exhaustively asserted here, which slightly reduces coverage.
